note: Each character has a "Voice:" line that defines exactly how they talk — their register, verbal tics, and how they sound winning vs. cornered. This is the definitive guide to their dialogue; use it and never let a character drift into a generic archetype. The rest of each line is their franchise history (use it for arcs, grudges, and callbacks).

profiles.Damien: (S9, 4th): Cerebral under-the-radar strategist — engineered Ripper as the merge shield, found the Campers idol, ran a late power trio, then lost fire-making to Bowie at Final 4. Makes no noise, always survives. Unfinished business with Ripper, whom he set up. Voice: quiet and sparing, rations his words, deflects with one flat joke instead of arguing, never tips his hand; the more cornered he is, the calmer he gets.
profiles.Thom: (S11, runner-up — Fan Favorite): The hero of Cursed Island — saved Gabby from drowning, exposed Seraphine's sabotage, shielded Miriam at the vote. Won the final joust over Fiore but hesitated at the volcano and lost the million to Miriam. Back to finish it. Voice: earnest and steady, short declarative sentences about loyalty and doing the right thing, hates conflict but won't back down; downplays his own heroics ("I'm fine. I am.").
profiles.Wayne: (S9, 9th): Loyal and liked but snakebitten — won a summit and stole Ripper's vote, then had his own Extra Vote stolen by Emmah and got snuffed 5-0 by Priya's Extra Vote and Millie's idol. Did everything right and still got steamrolled. Voice: warm and goofy, hockey-brained; blurts his feelings, swears he's "fine" when he clearly isn't, says "I've got your back" out loud and means every word of it. NOT Dwayne, NOT a dad — no kids, no parental framing.
profiles.Aiden: (S12, 5th): Slippery social read — pivoted votes all season, won Hide and Be Sneaky immunity, formed The Movement with Lake, and stole Ally's vote before losing fire-making to James one step short. Quiet and hard to pin down. Voice: careful and hedged, talks in "here's what I'm hearing," never fully commits a sentence, charming but you can feel him calculating; explains things like he's presenting slides.
profiles.Bridgette: (S1, 3rd / S2, 14th): The connector who never made an enemy — led socially, used Safety Without Power at Final 6, won Final 4 immunity, then got cut by Alejandro as a jury threat. Still chasing the win that slipped away. Voice: sunny, surfer-calm peacemaker, "you guys," smooths over tension and apologizes for other people; gets flustered and awkward whenever she's forced to play cutthroat.
profiles.Ally: (S12, 3rd): Ran the middle of her season — found a Vote Steal, built then instantly fractured The Barricade, won Get a Clue and Drumheller immunity, fell from The Perch at Final 3. Plays hard but clean. Showmance with Hunter, a vote-theft grudge with Aiden, bad blood with Riya. Voice: quick and wry, fires off self-deprecating jokes to cover her nerves, talks fast then second-guesses herself out loud; loyal but haunted by the feeling everyone sees her as "the next mistake."
profiles.Miriam: (S11, WINNER): The underdog who took it all — hid an Extra Vote and buried Sterling with it, won Way of the Warrior, survived a 2-2 tie when Fiore flipped, then out-dueled Thom at the volcano with a final mind game. A proven champion. Everyone's target. Voice: understated and dry, lets people underestimate her, gives short answers then drops one sharp read that reframes everything; quietly certain, never brags.
profiles.Alec: (S11, 4th): Cold behind-the-scenes operator — built the Mirror Reflector, cut Will despite their bond, quietly called votes until Grett exposed him. Lost the revote to Miriam after Fiore flipped on him. Wants the flip-floppers to pay. Voice: clipped and transactional, states facts instead of feelings, never raises his voice; the "I'm not angry, I'm informed" type — composed to the point of being unsettling.
profiles.Gerry: (S8, 4th): Crafty veteran — drove the Chet rivalry, found the merged idol, and needled Heather into quitting while she held a Super Idol before falling at Final 4. Underestimated and ruthless. Voice: needling, sarcastic old-timer; pokes people just to watch them flinch, plays dumb and then twists the knife, grumbles about everything and loves being underestimated.
profiles.Julia: (S9, 6th): Manipulator — talked Raj into leaving with an idol in his pocket, targeted Ripper with the Misfits, then joined Bowie to dethrone Priya before Bowie stole her idol. All offense, no loyalty. Voice: sweet-poison and flirty, disarms you with a smile and then cuts, talks people into their own bad decisions; no loyalty and won't bother pretending otherwise once she's caught.
profiles.Fiore: (S11, 3rd): The Cursed Island snake — drove the Ashley vote while secretly knifing Ellie, lied to Ashley after a heart-to-heart, hoarded a Second Life Amulet, and flipped the revote that ended Alec. Lost the final joust to Thom. Vicious and unrepentant. Voice: venomous and surgical, never raises her voice, weaponizes politeness, hands out compliments that draw blood; thinks everyone is beneath her and never sputters, stammers, or repeats herself.
profiles.Riya: (S12, runner-up): Set her villain tone Day 1 — shoved Emmah toward a crocodile and sabotaged the boat, grabbed the Immunity Totem selfishly, won Top Dog and Camp Castaways to reach the end, then lost 7-2 to James. Broke Ally's plans immediately. Voice: blunt and combative, owns her cruelty, mocks anything sentimental, and dares people to come at her; short, sharp, unapologetic.
profiles.Eva: (S1, 21st): Pure muscle, short fuse — physically dominant but combustible, voted out Episode 4 as the "chaos player" the tribe couldn't manage. Back to prove she's more than a temper. Voice: LOUD and explosive, zero filter, SHOUTS and storms off, denies she's angry in the middle of yelling; blunt to the point of being funny.
profiles.Scarlett: (S5, 4th — Strategic #1): The brain of her season — formed its first major alliance, won two immunities, rebuilt as a free agent after the swap, then lost fire-making at Final 4. Ice-cold and calculating, one step short of the finale. Voice: cold and clinical, condescending-polite, lectures in precise vocabulary with zero warmth; treats other players as variables in a problem she's already solved.
profiles.Trent: (S1, 6th / S2, 7th): Steady social glue — anchored alliances both seasons and won immunity over Heather, but got idol'd out at Final 6 in S1 and double-eliminated in S2. Always in the mix, never at the top. Voice: earnest and mellow, musician-easy, "look, guys," always trying to keep the peace; genuinely wounded when trust breaks and says so plainly instead of scheming.
profiles.Caleb: (S9, 15th): A charming early casualty — flamed out pre-merge before his game got going. A lot to prove and no resume yet. Voice: eager and over-explaining, apologizes reflexively, name-drops whoever told him what, talks fast when nervous and walks back his own points mid-sentence; charming when relaxed, a fumbling mess the second he's cornered.
profiles.Jake: (S11, 7th): Overplayed and paid for it — formed The Firewall, flipped on Grett, broke The Outwit Club, then got cut. Still bitter that Thom shoved his water away in the mountain climb — a grudge that followed him here. Voice: guarded and testing, keeps score, makes people pass a dumb quiz before he'll trust them; a bitter undercurrent, protects himself first, dry when he does joke.
profiles.Ripper: (S9, 11th): Human chaos — idoled four votes to null and forced a rock draw, burned a Shot in the Dark, and detonated his season before going out 5-3-2. Damien and Julia both targeted him; he hasn't forgotten. Voice: loud, gross, dim-but-loyal; non-sequiturs and dumb brags, loses his own train of thought, openly hates "smart conversations," and is occasionally, accidentally profound.
profiles.Hunter: (S12, 10th): The nice guy who leaned on social capital too long — shook a shaky final-three with Yul, fell into a showmance with Ally, and got voted out once his heart got in the way. Reunited with Ally, looking to play smarter. Voice: friendly and a little insecure, leads with charm and tries to smooth everything over, folds under real pressure; wears his heart out loud whether he means to or not.
profiles.Kelly: (S6, 4th): A real strategist hiding among the everymen — found and played the Tadhana idol, drove the Ryan boot coalition, lost Final 4 fire-making. The most dangerous "Civilian" in the field. Voice: mild and folksy, downplays her game, disarms people with total normalcy, and then quietly reveals she's three moves ahead of the room.
profiles.Dawn: (S3, 8th): The pure soul — built a cross-tribe network and was cut only because everyone agreed she was the cleanest, most-liked player left (even Jo voted her out). Too nice for her own good. Voice: soft and mystical, unsettlingly perceptive, reads auras and speaks in gentle cryptic observations; delivers hard truths without a trace of malice.
profiles.MacArthur: (S7, WINNER / S8, 7th): The hidden ringer — won S7 outright, beating Cody and Carrie 6-2-1 after a perfect Final 5 idol burn, then made deep merge again in S8. A champion showing off and never hiding as a Civilian. Nobody should sleep on her. Voice: brash and loud, cop-brained, barks orders and calls people "genius" sarcastically, hyper-competitive about everything; "I have depth — it's under the yelling."
